description: Network-based gaming console detection with LED color feedback on Raspberry Pi/Linux and ESP32

platform.os_requirements: Linux with arp, ping commands available; ESP32 with Arduino framework

setup: Install Python 3.9+ and dependencies: pip install -r requirements.txt
For ESP32: install Arduino IDE or PlatformIO, add ESP32 board support
Install ESP32 libraries: FastLED 3.10.3, ESPing 1.0.5, ESPmDNS
Configure config.json (Python) or config.h (ESP32) with network settings
pipeline.build: Native Python - no build step; ESP32 compiles via Arduino CLI
deploy: Python: copy files to RPi, run python3 main.py or use systemd service
ESP32: upload via Arduino IDE, esptool.py, or OTA (ArduinoOTA on port 3232)

sync_instructions.description: Guidelines for syncing features between Python (main) and ESP32 branches
principles: Each branch maintains independent implementation appropriate for its platform
Feature parity is goal, not exact code parity
Manifest updates should be mirrored to both branches
Detection logic (MAC_OUI, STATIC_LIST, PORT_PROBE, MDNS) should be consistent
Remote command protocol (COLOR:, RGB:, OFF, STATUS?) is shared and versioned
when_to_sync: Adding new supported brands and colors
Fixing bugs that apply to both platforms
UI/UX improvements to web config interface

sync_process.step2: Test thoroughly on that platform (compile + verify)
sync_process.step3: Port logic to other branch (adapt code, not copy-paste)
sync_process.step5: Update both manifests to reflect new feature
sync_process.step6: Commit with descriptive message mentioning both branches

do_not_sync: Language-specific patterns (Python threads vs Arduino loops)

python_components.main.py: Entry point, GameStateController with optional ESP32 remote mirror
python_components.scanner.py: Network device discovery via ARP table + static list
python_components.config_manager.py: Thread-safe singleton with atomic writes and deep merge
python_components.steam_detector.py: Steam detection via port probe or mDNS (zeroconf)
python_components.web_config.py: HTTP config UI with ConfigManager integration and ESP32 remote settings
python_components.esp32_client.py: TCP client for remote ESP32 color commands

esp32_components.esp32.ino: Main sketch — non-blocking loop, command server, captive portal, AP/STA switching, OTA
esp32_components.config.h: Hardware config, color definitions, OUI prefixes, Steam ports
esp32_components.config_manager.h: NVS-persisted config (WiFi creds, devices, command port, scan interval, brightness, fail counter)
esp32_components.led_controller.h: FastLED driver with CRGB color model
esp32_components.steam_detector.h: Multi-port + mDNS Steam detection with retries
esp32_components.network_scanner.h: Static device validator with IP format check
esp32_components.captive_portal.h: AP mode setup portal with web form, NVS persistence
esp32_components.web_ui.h: HTML/JS config UI with device management and command port settings
esp32_components.basic_auth.h: Session-based auth and CSRF token validation

architecture.flow: Scan (ARP + static) -> Liveness (ping) -> Steam (port probe / mDNS) -> Priority (last online wins) -> LED color + optional ESP32 remote mirror

detection.device_management: Web UI with JSON editor (Python) and REST API (ESP32)

remote_command_protocol.transport: TCP, one-shot connection per command, newline-delimited text

commands.COLOR:<name>: Set LED by named color (e.g. COLOR:blue)
commands.RGB:<r>,<g>,<b>: Set LED by RGB values 0-255 (e.g. RGB:255,0,0)

endpoints_python: GET / - Main configuration UI with JSON editor
endpoints_esp32: GET / - Web UI with device management and command port settings
POST /save - Add/edit device or set command port
GET /api/devices - List all devices as JSON
security_notes: Basic auth available on ESP32 (credentials in NVS)
CSRF protection via time-based tokens (see CSRF_PROTECTION.md)
No TLS by default - use on trusted networks or behind HTTPS proxy

ota.note: OTA uses ArduinoOTA; authenticate before flashing in production

main_branch.python/: All Python source code and tests

ESP32_branch.root: Python files, config.json, requirements.txt, .github/, AI/, README.md

v2.3.1: Moved ESP32 C++ files into esp32/ directory on ESP32 branch
Updated CI workflow for branch-aware test/compile paths
v2.3.0: Added esp32_client.py for TCP remote color control of ESP32 from Python
Added dual-mode LED control in Python main.py (local + optional ESP32 mirror)
Added basic authentication framework for ESP32 web UI (basic_auth.h)
Added captive portal to ESP32 with NVS-persisted WiFi credentials
Expanded test coverage: test_scanner.py, test_liveness.py, test_integration.py, test_esp32_client.py
Fixed config_manager.h with full NVS methods (WiFi, scan interval, brightness, fail counter)
Fixed config_manager.py with deep merge for nested dict updates
Updated web_config.py with ConfigManager integration and ESP32 remote settings
Updated esp32.ino with non-blocking WiFi reconnect, OTA, auth, and captive portal
Updated project manifest v2.3.0 with remote_command and ota documentation
Added CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) with Arduino CLI compile
Updated README.md with full API docs and remote control protocol
Added lightchanger.service systemd unit for RPi deployment
v2.4.0: Restructured ESP32 branch: Python files moved to python/, C++ files in esp32/, root main.py delegates
Fixed _recv_line in esp32_client.py: added max_bytes=4096 guard to prevent hang
Fixed scanner.py ARP regex for macOS single-digit hex MACs
Fixed scanner.py to honor detection_mode config (HYBRID/STATIC_LIST/AUTO)
Fixed steam_detector.py _resolve_mdns timeout to match resolve_mdns_multi (ms consistent)
Wired ConfigManager singleton into GameStateController (thread-safe, atomic writes)
Removed dead get_mac_address() method from scanner.py
Added 15 tests for config_manager.py (singleton, load, get, update, persist, reload)
Updated project manifest v2.4.0 with directory structure, CI paths, and architecture docs
